awd · Gotchas

Union Types and State Management

← Back to Course Package
⚠️ The Union Type Math Trap
TypeScript prevents operations on union types like 'number | string' even if you know the value is currently a number. If you skip a type guard like 'typeof === number', your code will fail to compile despite the logic appearing sound.
⚠️ The NaN Number Paradox
The 'number' type in TypeScript includes 'NaN', meaning a variable can be technically valid but mathematically broken. If you don't follow a 'parseFloat' with an 'isNaN' check, your application will process invalid calculations without throwing a TypeScript error.
⚠️ The ESM Module Requirement
Modern TypeScript syntax requires the '"type": "module"' field in your package.json to function correctly in Node.js. Omitting this setting causes cryptic runtime errors regarding 'import' statements, even if your TypeScript code is perfectly written.
⚠️ The Config Bloat Confusion
Running 'tsc --init' generates a file with hundreds of commented-out lines that overwhelm beginners. Using the default bloated config makes it difficult to identify which settings are actually active, leading to configuration conflicts and hidden compiler behaviors.
